about.bio: I started coding at 12 by building mini-games on platforms such as Game Creator and Roblox. What began as curiosity evolved into a deeper interest in software engineering, system design, and creative digital experiences. Beyond technology, I am passionate about the financial sector — including stock trading and investment — and I enjoy exploring how technology can optimize financial decision-making. I also love traveling and going to the gym, and I aspire to become a software engineer who designs impactful and innovative digital solutions.

projects.title: Telegram AI Chat Bot
projects.description: Built a Python-based Telegram AI assistant with a modular multi-agent architecture, enabling agents to communicate with one another and form a small autonomous AI ecosystem. Features include scheduled prompting for automated daily briefings, real-time stock tracking with the latest market news, and task automation workflows for retrieving stock insights and job listings. Powered by the Anthropic API and deployed on Render, with market data sourced from Finnhub and Yahoo Finance.

projects.title: Automated Projected Appointment
projects.description: Developed an automated template filler using Excel and VBA for projected RSAF officer appointments that helps visualize and define timetable boundaries, streamlining scheduling and improving planning efficiency.

projects.title: Automated CV Display System
projects.description: Developed a CV display system for RSAF HR department that allows viewing of crucial personnel information such as photo, rank, past appointments, age, and more using Excel and VBA automation, improving efficiency in managing records.

projects.title: Data Sorting Application
projects.description: Developed a data sorting application for mVizn Pte Ltd. with built-in image editing tools using Java and MySQL. Designed and implemented core features for organizing data efficiently while allowing users to edit and manage images on windows platform. Improving company's data sorting efficiency by 40%.

projects.title: Fruit Image Classifier
projects.description: Developed a machine learning image classification system using Python and TensorFlow to distinguish between bananas and oranges. Implemented a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) with image preprocessing, model training, and evaluation to improve accuracy. Built an Android mobile application to integrate the trained model, allowing users to upload or capture images for real-time fruit classification.

projects.title: Library Inventory System
projects.description: Developed a Java desktop application using JavaFX and MySQL to manage library inventory, borrowing records, and user accounts. Implemented OOP design patterns, fine calculation logic, search and filtering features, admin dashboards, and statistical reporting. Integrated the JavaMail API to trigger automated overdue notifications, improving return compliance and operational efficiency.

projects.title: Medicine Dispenser Project
projects.description: Led the development of a medicine dispenser utilizing the PIC18 microcontroller interfaced with motors, LCD, displays, keypads, and Bluetooth modules to assist elderly individuals with medication management.

projects.title: Virtual Marketplace
projects.description: Worked in a team to build an e-commerce web application from scratch using HTML, PHP, and AstraDB (NoSQL), designed as a virtual marketplace. I handled the UI/UX design and developed key features like user authentication, product listings, and a real-time chat system for buyer-seller communication. Used GitHub to manage branches and track different versions of the codebase, ensuring smooth collaboration and version control.

projects.title: Mental Health Support Mobile App
projects.description: Developed a mental health support mobile application using Android Studio with Java, integrating Firebase services for cloud database storage. Implemented features such as mood tracking, journaling, and secure data management to support daily mental wellness and habit monitoring.

clubs.name: SP Canoe Polo
clubs.description:
I spent my two years in the Singapore Polytechnic (SP) Canoe Polo Executive Committee balancing the demands of high-level leadership with the physical grind of the sport. As President and Vice President, I helped the club achieve an Excellence Award in 2022 by setting ambitious SMART goals and overhauling our recruitment. On the water, I pushed myself just as hard, competing in multiple university-level tournaments where I secured two medals and eventually earned the honor of representing Singapore on the Under-21 National Team.

One of my biggest challenges was stepping up as the Organizing Chairman for the SP Canoe Polo Opens Championship. I was responsible for the entire timeline, managing a team that handled everything from logistics and sponsorship to the refereeing panel. I also made it a point to share the technical side of the sport through boat repair workshops and led 'Sports for Life' sessions to encourage fitness across the campus.  

Beyond the competition and the gear, I wanted our club to make a real impact. I collaborated with external partners to host a community service event for injured migrant workers, giving them a guided tour of Gardens by the Bay. Whether I was representing the country or managing a committee, my goal was to leave the club with a stronger legacy and a deeper connection to the community.

clubs.name: HQ RSAF NSF Council
clubs.description:
I currently serve as a leader in the Headerquarters Republic Of Singapore Airforce (HQ RSAF) NSF Council, where my main goal is to make the NS journey more engaging and meaningful for my fellow airmen. A big part of my role involves mentoring my EXCO team and handling our internal operations, while also working closely with senior leadership in the Republic of Singapore Air Force to represent NSF perspectives and bridge the gap between ground experiences and HQ decisions. From welcoming new batches of recruits during induction talks to planning our yearly direction, I'm motivated by the idea of building a stronger and more connected NSF community. 

As the Event Head, I focus on creating activities that are both enjoyable and meaningful. I've coordinated large-scale learning visits to places like the Singapore Navy Museum, the Indian Heritage Centre, and the Singapore City Gallery. I handled everything from logistics and transport planning to the overall program flow. Beyond just organizing outings, these experiences were designed to help NSFs better appreciate our national identity and understand the wider defence ecosystem. By balancing advocacy work with hands-on event execution, I aim to make sure every NSF feels welcomed, supported, and valued from the very start of their journey.

clubs.name: EEE Club
clubs.description: My involvement in leadership started early at Singapore Polytechnic (SP) within the Electrical & Electronic Engineering (EEE) Club, where I served on the Welfare Committee. During the height of the COVID-19 pandemic, I focused on maintaining student morale by coordinating the distribution of exam welfare packs to help my peers through a stressful academic season. I also stepped up as a Group Leader for our student camps, which was a unique challenge during that period. I had to find creative ways to mentor and engage a fresh batch of students, ensuring they felt welcomed into the SP community despite the social distancing hurdles and the shift to a hybrid campus life.

achievements.title: Best Airman of the Month
achievements.description: Awarded Best Airman of the Month at HQ RSAF for outstanding performance and leadership.

achievements.title: ST Engineering Prize
achievements.description: Awarded upon graduating from Singapore Polytechnic, the ST Engineering Prize is given to top students who have demonstrated exceptional academic performance and potential.

achievements.title: Edusave Academic Award 2024
achievements.description: Awarded to students who are within the top 25% of their school's level and course in terms of academic performance, have demonstrated good conduct

achievements.description: Awarded to students who are within the top 25% of their school's level and course in terms of academic performance, have demonstrated good conduct
